title: Containerizing Shiny Applications with shiny2docker
text: shiny2docker R package designed streamline process containerizing Shiny applications using Docker. vignette demonstrates generate Dockerfile Shiny application, customize , configure GitLab CI build deploy Docker image.

title: Containerizing Shiny Applications with shiny2docker
text: main function, shiny2docker(), automates creation Dockerfile. following: Utilizes renv.lock file capture R package dependencies. Generates .dockerignore file exclude unnecessary files, reducing build time image size. Creates Dockerfile includes instructions install system dependencies, R packages, launch Shiny app. generate Dockerfile current directory, simply run: Alternatively, can specify custom paths Shiny application, renv.lock file, output Dockerfile:
code: library(shiny2docker)  # Generate a Dockerfile in the current directory shiny2docker(path = ".") library(shiny2docker)  # Generate a Dockerfile with specific paths shiny2docker(   path = "path/to/your/app",   lockfile = "path/to/your/app/renv.lock",   output = "path/to/your/app/Dockerfile" )

title: Containerizing Shiny Applications with shiny2docker
text: shiny2docker() function returns object class dockerfiler. object can manipulated add custom instructions writing Dockerfile disk. example, may want set environment variable:
code: # Create the dockerfiler object docker_obj <- shiny2docker()  # Add environment variables docker_obj$add_after(  cmd = "ENV ARROW_WITH_S3=1",  after = 1) docker_obj$add_after(  cmd = "ENV ARROW_S3=ON",after = 1)   # Write the updated Dockerfile to disk docker_obj$write("Dockerfile")

title: Containerizing Shiny Applications with shiny2docker
text: package provides set_gitlab_ci() function simplify process configuring GitLab CI pipeline. pipeline designed build Docker image push GitLab container registry. .gitlab-ci.yml file place, can integrate GitLab CI/CD automate Docker image build deployment process.
code: # Copy the .gitlab-ci.yml file to the current directory set_gitlab_ci(path = ".")  # Specify runner tags set_gitlab_ci(path = ".", tags = c("shiny_build", "prod"))

title: Containerizing Shiny Applications with shiny2docker
text: package also provides set_github_action() function, simplifies process configuring GitHub Actions pipeline. pipeline designed build Docker image push created image GitHub Container Registry. docker-build.yml file place, can integrate GitHub Actions automate Docker image build deployment process, allowing continuously build deploy containerized Shiny applications.
code: # Copy the docker-build.yml file to the .github/workflows/ directory set_github_action(path = ".")

title: Containerizing Shiny Applications with shiny2docker
text: shiny2docker simplifies containerizing Shiny applications automating Dockerfile creation CI configuration. integrating renv dependency management providing customizable dockerfiler object, offers flexible workflow deploying Shiny apps containerized environments. details, please refer package documentation visit dockerfiler GitHub repository.

title: Generate Dockerfiles for Shiny Applications
text: shiny2docker R package designed streamline process containerizing Shiny applications using Docker. automating generation essential Docker files managing R dependencies renv, shiny2docker simplifies deployment Shiny apps, ensuring reproducibility consistency across different environments.

title: Generate Dockerfiles for Shiny Applications
text: Automated Dockerfile Generation: Quickly generate Dockerfiles tailored Shiny applications. main function, shiny2docker(), creates Dockerfile using renv.lock file capture R package dependencies. Dependency Management: Utilize renv manage restore R package dependencies. lockfile exist, shiny2docker() automatically create one production using attachment::create_renv_for_prod function. Customizability: shiny2docker() function returns dockerfiler object can manipulated using dockerfiler’s methods writing file. enables advanced users customize Dockerfile better suit needs. See dockerfiler details. GitLab CI Integration: set_gitlab_ci() function, can easily configure GitLab CI pipeline. function copies pre-configured gitlab-ci.yml file package project directory. provided CI configuration designed build Docker image push created image GitLab container registry, thereby streamlining continuous integration deployment workflows. GitHub Actions Integration: set_github_action() function, can quickly set GitHub Actions pipeline. function copies pre-configured docker-build.yml file package .github/workflows/ directory project. provided CI configuration designed build Docker image push created image GitHub Container Registry, facilitating automated builds deployments GitHub.

title: Generate Dockerfiles for Shiny Applications
text: can install production version CRAN : can install development version shiny2docker GitHub :

what: Generate a Dockerfile for a Shiny Application
title: Generate Dockerfiles for Shiny Applications
text: Use shiny2docker() function automatically generate Dockerfile based application’s dependencies.
code: library(shiny2docker)  # Generate Dockerfile in the current directory shiny2docker(path = ".")  # Generate Dockerfile with a specific renv.lock and output path shiny2docker(path = "path/to/shiny/app",              lockfile = "path/to/shiny/app/renv.lock",              output = "path/to/shiny/app/Dockerfile")  # Further manipulate the Dockerfile object before writing to disk docker_obj <- shiny2docker() docker_obj$ENV("MY_ENV_VAR", "value") docker_obj$write("Dockerfile")

what: Configure GitLab CI for Docker Builds
title: Generate Dockerfiles for Shiny Applications
text: set_gitlab_ci() function allows quickly set GitLab CI pipeline build Docker image push GitLab container registry.
code: library(shiny2docker)  # Copy the .gitlab-ci.yml file to the current directory set_gitlab_ci()  # Specify runner tags set_gitlab_ci(tags = c("shiny_build", "prod"))

what: Configure GitHub Actions for Docker Builds
title: Generate Dockerfiles for Shiny Applications
text: new set_github_action() function allows quickly set GitHub Actions pipeline build Docker image push GitHub Container Registry. docker-build.yml file place, can integrate GitHub Actions automate Docker image build deployment process.
code: library(shiny2docker)  # Copy the docker-build.yml file to the .github/workflows/ directory set_github_action(path = ".")

title: Generate Dockerfiles for Shiny Applications
text: Prepare Shiny Application: Ensure Shiny app located folder necessary files (e.g., app.R ui.R server.R). Generate Dockerfile: Run shiny2docker() create Dockerfile (.dockerignore file) project directory. Dockerfile include instructions install system dependencies, R packages, launch Shiny app. Set Continuous Integration (Optional): use GitLab, run set_gitlab_ci() copy pre-configured GitLab CI file project. CI configuration handle Docker image build deployment GitLab’s container registry. use GitHub, run set_github_action(path = ".") copy pre-configured GitHub Actions file project. CI configuration build Docker image push GitHub Container Registry. Deploy Application: Use Docker build run image, integrate chosen CI/CD service automated deployments.

title: shiny2docker — shiny2docker
text: Generate Dockerfile Shiny Application

title: shiny2docker — shiny2docker
code: shiny2docker(   path = ".",   lockfile = file.path(path, "renv.lock"),   output = file.path(path, "Dockerfile"),   FROM = "rocker/geospatial",   AS = NULL,   sysreqs = TRUE,   repos = c(CRAN = "https://cran.rstudio.com/"),   expand = FALSE,   extra_sysreqs = NULL,   use_pak = FALSE,   user = NULL,   dependencies = NA,   sysreqs_platform = "ubuntu",   folder_to_exclude = c("renv"),   renv_version )

title: shiny2docker — shiny2docker
text: path Character. Path folder containing Shiny application (e.g., app.R ui.R server.R) along necessary files. lockfile Character. Path renv.lock file specifies R package dependencies. renv.lock file exist, created production using attachment::create_renv_for_prod function. output Character. Path generated Dockerfile. Defaults "Dockerfile". Docker image start . Default rocker/geospatial Dockerfile. Default NULL. sysreqs boolean. TRUE, Dockerfile contain sysreq installation. repos character. URL(s) repositories use options("repos"). expand boolean. TRUE system requirement RUN line. extra_sysreqs character vector. Extra debian system requirements. installed apt-get install. use_pak boolean. TRUE use pak deal dependencies  renv::restore(). FALSE default user Name user specify Dockerfile USER instruction. Default NULL, case user image used. dependencies kinds dependencies install. commonly one following values: NA: required (hard) dependencies, TRUE: required dependencies plus optional development dependencies, FALSE: install dependencies. (might end non-working package, /installation might fail.) sysreqs_platform System requirements platform.ubuntu default. NULL,  current platform used. Can : "ubuntu-22.04" needed fit Operating System. debian ubuntu based images supported folder_to_exclude Folder exclude scan detect packages renv_version character. Optional. Forwarded dockerfiler::dock_from_renv(). default (parameter omitted), renv version recorded renv.lock file used. Set NULL install latest available renv configured repos (faster build, remotes dependency).

title: shiny2docker — shiny2docker
text: object class dockerfiler, representing generated Dockerfile. object can manipulated using dockerfiler functions written disk.

title: shiny2docker — shiny2docker
text: Automate creation Dockerfile tailored deploying Shiny applications. manages R dependencies using renv, generates .dockerignore file optimize Docker build process, leverages dockerfiler package allow customization Dockerfile object writing disk.

title: shiny2docker — shiny2docker
code: # \donttest{   temp_dir <- tempfile("shiny2docker_example_")   dir.create(temp_dir)   example_app <- system.file("dummy_app", package = "shiny2docker")   file.copy(example_app, temp_dir, recursive = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE    app_path <- file.path(temp_dir, "dummy_app")   if (requireNamespace("rstudioapi", quietly = TRUE) &&   rstudioapi::isAvailable()) {     rstudioapi::filesPaneNavigate(app_path)   }    docker_obj <- shiny2docker::shiny2docker(path = app_path) #> Loading required namespace: renv #> ℹ No DESCRIPTION file found #> ℹ we wil parse qmd files,Rmd files and R scripts from  /tmp/RtmpLbcJPi/shiny2docker_example_19e82d4f8ed3/dummy_app . #> All required packages are installed #> ✔ create renv.lock at /tmp/RtmpLbcJPi/shiny2docker_example_19e82d4f8ed3/dummy_app/renv.lock #> The following package(s) will be updated in the lockfile: #>  #> # RSPM ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #> - R6            [* -> 2.6.1] #> - Rcpp          [* -> 1.1.1-1.1] #> - base64enc     [* -> 0.1-6] #> - bslib         [* -> 0.10.0] #> - cachem        [* -> 1.1.0] #> - cli           [* -> 3.6.6] #> - commonmark    [* -> 2.0.0] #> - digest        [* -> 0.6.39] #> - fastmap       [* -> 1.2.0] #> - fontawesome   [* -> 0.5.3] #> - fs            [* -> 2.1.0] #> - glue          [* -> 1.8.1] #> - htmltools     [* -> 0.5.9] #> - httpuv        [* -> 1.6.17] #> - jquerylib     [* -> 0.1.4] #> - jsonlite      [* -> 2.0.0] #> - later         [* -> 1.4.8] #> - lifecycle     [* -> 1.0.5] #> - magrittr      [* -> 2.0.5] #> - memoise       [* -> 2.0.1] #> - mime          [* -> 0.13] #> - otel          [* -> 0.2.0] #> - promises      [* -> 1.5.0] #> - rappdirs      [* -> 0.3.4] #> - remotes       [* -> 2.5.0] #> - rlang         [* -> 1.2.0] #> - sass          [* -> 0.4.10] #> - shiny         [* -> 1.13.0] #> - sourcetools   [* -> 0.1.7-2] #> - withr         [* -> 3.0.2] #> - xtable        [* -> 1.8-8] #>  #> The version of R recorded in the lockfile will be updated: #> - R             [* -> 4.6.0] #>  #> - Lockfile written to "/tmp/RtmpLbcJPi/shiny2docker_example_19e82d4f8ed3/dummy_app/renv.lock". #> renv version = the most up to date version in the repos #> ℹ Please wait while we compute system requirements... #> Fetching system dependencies for 31 package(s) records. #> Warning: `lang()` is deprecated as of rlang 0.2.0. Please use `call2()` instead. #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. #> ℹ Loading metadata database #> ✔ Loading metadata database ... done #>  #> ✔ Done    print(list.files(app_path,all.files = TRUE,no.. = TRUE)) #> [1] ".dockerignore" "Dockerfile"    "app.R"         "renv.lock"        # Further manipulate the Dockerfile object  docker_obj$add_after(    cmd = "ENV ENV \'MY_ENV_VAR\'=\'value\'",    after = 3  )  docker_obj$write(file.path(app_path, "Dockerfile")) # }

title: shiny2docker 0.0.4
text: shiny2docker() gains renv_version parameter, forwarded dockerfiler::dock_from_renv(). Set NULL bootstrap latest available renv (skipping remotes dependency entirely). Minimum dockerfiler version bumped >= 0.2.6 renv_version argument.

title: shiny2docker 0.0.1
text: shiny2docker Generate Dockerfile Shiny Application. function automates creation Dockerfile tailored deploying Shiny applications managing R dependencies renv, generating .dockerignore file optimize Docker build process, leveraging dockerfiler package customization. Additionally, uses attachment::create_renv_for_prod create lockfile one missing. set_gitlab_ci Configure GitLab CI pipeline Docker builds. function copies gitlab-ci.yml file provided package specified directory. configuration designed build Docker image push created image GitLab container registry.
